summary:
Articulating paper is most effective for detecting occlusal clearance because it offers immediate, reliable feedback on contact points under function. This makes it superior to other methods such as visual assessment or wax chew-in when evaluating occlusal fit during onlay placement. 

The above summary contains redundant information; revise only what needs revision while preserving all essential content.

Summary: Articulating paper detects occlusal clearance effectively due to its ability to provide real-time, accurate feedback on contact points under functional loads. Among common techniques used clinically, this method is considered optimal for assessing occlusal relationship during onlay restoration.
